title: MERS-CoV Mpro large scale purification protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"f7euh","text":"This protocol details the expression and purification of MERS-CoV Mpro construct bearing a N-terminal His-SUMO tag at large scale (\u003e6L).\n","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"e7vro","text":"Construct / plasmid resource-name: MERS-Mpro construct bearing a N-terminal SUMO-His tag.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":39}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

Thus no imidazole was included in the wash buffer to reduce product loss.","type":"unstyled"}],"entityMap":{}}}}}
steps: Transform MERS-CoV Mpro addgene Catalog #228646 into BL21(DE3) and store a glycerol stock of this at -80 °C MERS Mpro plasmid was transformed into BL21(DE3)-RR strain along other constructs, and stored at -80 °C .
Perform IMAC to extract target protein from the lysed cell mixture

Wash the column with 10 CV of wash buffer twice. Allow wash buffer to pass through completely between washes. This is to remove non-specific, weak binding of contaminant proteins from the resin for a cleaner elution. Collect washes separately for SDS-PAGE analysis.

Using the AKTA Pure system: Inject the sample onto a 5mL sample loop. Run the sample down HiLoad 16/60 Superdex 200 pg gel filtration column at 1mL/min in gel filtration buffer, collecting 1mL aliquots.
Aliquot into appropriate volumes for future usage to minimise freeze/thaw cycles. Flash-freeze in liquid nitrogen, and store at -80 °C until required.
Lyse cell pellet
Lyse by sonication 00:00:04, On 00:00:12, Off for a total 'on' time of 00:07:00 at 50% amplitude to fully rupture the cells. Ensure pellet is °C during sonication to prevent overheating.
Resuspend the equilibrated resin with some binding buffer and add to the supernatant bottle. Incubate the resin with the supernatant for 00:30:00 while rotating or otherwise mixing gently at 4 °C

Load the resin/supernatant mix back onto the gravity flow column, retaining the FT separately for SDS-PAGE analysis. For SDS-PAGE samples, mix 15uL sample with 5uL 4x sample buffer, supplemented with 10mM DTT.
Elute the protein with 1.5 CV of elution buffer.

Repeat step 6.5 once more, collecting a total of 2 separate elution fractions. This is to ensure maximum retrieval of protein from the resin. The total protein concentration of the elutions are measured by Nanodrop. Although still a mixture, A280 value can give an estimate of the protein content, which will determine how much protease need to be added to remove the affinity tag.
CV - column volume, total volume of resin in a column IMAC - immobilised metal affinity chromatography FT - flow through

Run SDS-PAGE of all samples from total lysis supernatant to final elution. Stain gel with protein staining solution Coomasssie Blue and determine which fractions contain the target protein by finding the band corresponding to the target molecular weight. The target protein is expected to be present mostly in the elution samples, although small amounts may be found in the FT and washes. If that is not the case, then further troubleshooting is required.
Purify sample further by size exclusion chromatography.

Dispense 5 mL Nickle affinity resin Ni Sepharose 6 FF - Cytiva into a gravity flow column. Equilibrate resin by first rinsing with ~ 10 CV distilled water, then ~ 10 CV binding buffer to remove the storage solution.
Centrifuge the lysed cells for 38000 x g, 4 °C, 1 hour to remove insoluble cell debris, and collect supernatant in a bottle 4 °C

See Materials tab for buffer compositions. Before tag cleavage: MW=45.382 kDa E (assume all Cys reduced)=48360 mM-1cm-1 PI=5.92 After tag cleavage: MW=33.330 kDa E (assume all Cys reduced)=43890 mM-1cm-1 PI=5.86 These values are determined by Expasy ProtParam Thaw and resuspend the pellet in ~7mL of lysis buffer per g of pellet. Stir gently with magnetic stir bar at Room temperature for 00:30:00 to allow lysozyme and bezonase to start breaking down cell components.
Elution de-salting, tag cleavage and reverse IMAC

The three elutions are pooled and desalted using HiPrep 26/10 deasalting columns, run on AKTA pure at the maximum flow rate of 10mL/min. This is to reduce imidazole concentration in the sample. High concentration of imidazole will inhibit protease activity during tag cleavage and removal.
For tag removal, His-SENP1 is added in 1:100 ratio to the total protein content of the desalted sample, as determined by nanodrop. The mixture is left at 4 °C Overnight

In morning, pour the cleavage mixture over the washed resin and collect FT. This step will remove the cleaved tag and any uncleaved target from the sample. If the protease used is His-tagged, then the protease is removed from sample too.
Wash rIMAC resin with 2 CV wash buffer to remove any target protein still bound to the resin. Take samples of the FT and wash, characterise content by SDS-PAGE
Concentrate all fractions of the rIMAC containing the target protein in spin concentrators of the appropriate MWCO, to a final volume of under 5 mL .

Remove any solid aggregates from the sample by centrifugation at 17200 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes , then immediatly draw up the supernatant with a 5mL syringe and a blunt-tip fill needle, taking care not to disturb the pellet. This is to remove as much solid particles from the injection sample as possible, so as to not clog the in-line filter or frit of the column.

From the chromatogram, fraction E1-F8 analyse by SDS-PAGE. Chromatogram for MERS-Mpro SEC SDS-PAGE analysis of SEC fraction E1-F8. Fractions E5-E12 were pooled as they contain majority target protein in comparison to contaminants. Red arrow indicated the position of bands corresponding to the target protein.

Take the fractions that contain the target protein, which in this case are fraction E1-F6. Concentrate the final sample in Vivaspin 6 10kda MWCO centrifugal concentrator until the concentration reaches > 33 mg/mL or 1 millimolar (mM) . Take 1 µL of the final sample for SDS-PAGE, and another for mass spectroscopy (MS). SDS-PAGE of the final purified MERS-Mpro construct. The higher molecular weight bands are likely polymeric forms of the target that failed to be monomerized during SDS-PAGE sample preparation. MS will confirm the purity of the sample. Intact MS result of the final sample. Major peak molecular weight (33330.673 Da) agrees with the expected weight (33330.29 Da), confirming the purity of the sample.

Wash used IMAC resin with 10CV of base buffer, and leave in the column submerged in a small amount of base buffer such that the resin is kept moist. This washed IMAC resin will later be reused for reverse IMAC (rIMAC)

(Optional) elute rIMAC resin with 2 CV elution buffer to confirm if the protein shows non-specific binding to the resin used. This will help determine if the protein is "sticky" to the Ni resin matrix material, and help in further troubleshooting if the final yield is lower than expected. SDS-PAGE analysis of IMAC and cleavage fractions. The higher molecular weight band, highlighted with red arrow, agrees with the size of cleaved target protein, while the lower band corresponds to the size of the cleaved SUMO tag (12.372 kDa but band appears at ~15kDa on the SDS-PAGE gels used)
